title: T-022 — Optimistic locking on wo_executions to prevent dual-completion (R2)
prompt:
# T-022 — Optimistic locking on wo_executions

PRD: 08-PRODUCTION-PRD.md §13 R2

## Goal
Add optimistic locking via wo_executions.updated_at semantics so that two concurrent COMPLETE attempts cannot both succeed (R2: concurrent WO completion conflicts).

## Implementation contract
Update complete-wo, pause-wo, resume-wo to UPDATE wo_executions SET ... WHERE wo_id=:id AND updated_at = :expected_updated_at. If 0 rows affected → return 409 {error:'concurrent_modification', code:'CONFLICT_OPTIMISTIC_LOCK'}. Each route handler reads expected_updated_at on entry to the transaction and passes to the service. Add automated test simulating two concurrent /complete calls — exactly one must return 200 and one must return 409.

## Files (scope)
- packages/api-services/production/complete-wo.ts [modify]
- packages/api-services/production/pause-wo.ts [modify]
- packages/api-services/production/resume-wo.ts [modify]
- packages/api-services/production/__tests__/optimistic-lock-concurrent.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given two concurrent /complete calls on same WO, when both run, then exactly one returns 200 and the other returns 409 code=CONFLICT_OPTIMISTIC_LOCK
2. Given single sequential /complete call, when run, then 200 success without lock conflict
3. Given the second call retries with refreshed updated_at, when called after the first commits, then it observes 409 invalid_state_transition (already COMPLETED) — proving lock semantics correctly chained with state machine

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/api-services/production/__tests__/optimistic-lock-concurrent.test.ts using Promise.all on two service calls before optimistic-lock change is added
- Run 'pnpm --filter api-services test optimistic-lock-concurrent'

## Risk red lines
- Do not switch to pessimistic FOR UPDATE (would serialize unnecessarily)
- Do not omit updated_at trigger maintaining now() on UPDATE
- Do not allow silent overwrite (must surface 409)

pipeline_inputs.description: Optimistic locking on wo_executions.updated_at to mitigate R2 concurrent WO completion conflicts.
pipeline_inputs.details: Each transitional service (complete/pause/resume) reads updated_at, runs UPDATE ... WHERE updated_at=:expected. Trigger maintains updated_at=now() on UPDATE. Concurrent test forces both to request the same expected_updated_at; only one succeeds.
out_of_scope: Do not change wo_executions schema beyond updated_at trigger
Do not refactor unrelated service code
acceptance_criteria: Given two concurrent /complete on same WO, when both run, then exactly one returns 200 and the other returns 409 code=CONFLICT_OPTIMISTIC_LOCK
Given single sequential /complete, when run, then 200 success
Given retry after first commits, when called, then observes 409 invalid_state_transition (state machine takes over)
test_strategy: RED: concurrent test using Promise.all asserting 200+409 split before lock change
risk_red_lines: Do not switch to FOR UPDATE
Do not omit updated_at maintenance trigger
